sections.paragraph:
ἀμετανόητος, ον (s. μετανοέω; mostly [Lucian, Vett. Val., Plotinus] pass. ‘irrevocable’, so also in pap) act. unrepentant Ro 2:5 (cp. TestGad 7:5; Epict., Fgm. 25 Sch.).—DELG s.v. νόος. M-M.

Lucian Lucian , II a.d.—List 5

Plotinus Plotinus , III a.d.—List 5

pass. pass. = passive (either of grammatical form or of passive experience); also used in reference to literary portion=passage

pap pap = papyrus, -yri

act. act. = active

  1. cp. = compare, freq. in ref. to citation fr. ancient texts

TestGad TestGad = Testament of Gad, s. Test12Patr—List 2

Epict Epict , various works, I–II a.d.—List 5

Fgm. Fgm. = fragment, fragmentary

Sch. Sch. = TSchermann, Prophetarum vitae fabulosae—List 5

DELG DELG = PChantraine, Dictionnaire étymologique de la langue grecque—List 6

s.v. s.v. = sub voce (under the word, look up the word)

M-M M-M = JMoulton/GMilligan, Vocabulary of Greek Testament—Lists 4, 6

Arndt, William ; Danker, Frederick W. ; Bauer, Walter: A Greek-English Lexicon of the New Testament and Other Early Christian Literature. 3rd ed. Chicago : University of Chicago Press, 2000, S. 53
